frame_generation: fixed-q linear interpolation between bracketing nonlinear profiles on a uniform phase grid
layout: left panel: phase-local power diagnostics versus r/R_sun; right column top: photosphere radial velocity curve; right column bottom: photosphere luminosity curve
zone_boundary_smoothing: ionization-zone boundaries are converted from temperature spans to q spans, then the q-space zone centers and widths are smoothed over phase with a low-order periodic Fourier fit before being mapped to the displayed coordinate

guide_label_source.heating_cooling_trigger: sign of dT/dphase for the photosphere
guide_label_source.expanding_contracting_trigger: sign of dR/dphase for the photosphere

component_definitions.heating_total: phase-local gas heating rate, -d(Lr + Lc)/dm - C, with positive meaning net heating of the gas reservoir
component_definitions.heating_radiative: radiative contribution to the heating rate, -dLr/dm, with positive meaning radiative heating
component_definitions.heating_convective: convective contribution to the heating rate, -dLc/dm, with positive meaning convective heating
component_definitions.pdv_power: phase-local specific mechanical-work rate, (P + P_av) d(1/rho)/dt, with positive meaning expansion work done by the gas and negative meaning contraction/compression

pdv_generation.method: phase-weighted periodic Fourier fit of shell specific volume, effective pressure, and rsp_Eq across the nonlinear cycle, with analytic dV/dt

phase_curve_break_reason: phase-ordered profiles cross the chronological cycle boundary here; the plotted phase curves are broken at these phases so an unconverged cycle is not connected by a synthetic spike

photosphere_visualization.style: dark-mode limb-darkened spheres with a luminosity/temperature-colored sphere on the luminosity panel and a radial-velocity-colored sphere on the radial-velocity panel
photosphere_visualization.limb_darkening_law: phase-dependent quadratic limb darkening with coefficients parameterized by photosphere Teff, log g, and inferred [Fe/H]
photosphere_visualization.phase_marker_scaling: marker diameter scales with the same radius_scale = R/R_ref factor used for the sphere zoom
